reasoning: The document shows significant evidence of content loss and structural failure, specifically with multiple instructional slides (15, 21, 29, 30, 32) rendered as empty sections in the HTML. This is corroborated by a low visual fidelity score (0.50) and a programmatic recall (0.821) that suggests missing text, as well as the specific omission of 33% of the contact information section.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: In the 'Get in touch' section (page 34), significant contact information is missing. The source lists 'Web: www.ablr360.com', 'Call: 833-294-2257', and 'Email: kim.casey@ablr360.com'. The output only includes the 'Ablr' name and the phone number. Out of the six lines of text in this section (Heading, Name, Web, Phone, Email, Closing), two lines (33%) are omitted. Per the guidelines, an omission of more than 25% of a section's text is a major defect.

The HTML output exhibits major layout discrepancies where several information-rich slides from the PDF are rendered as nearly empty sections. In the source PDF, slides 15, 21, 29, 30, and 32 contain vital instructional text and screenshots. In the converted HTML, these sections consist almost entirely of headings with no following content (neither the text nor the images). This fundamentally alters the visual organization and flow of information, making the document feel incomplete and preventin
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 15 ('Creating a Keyboard Shortcut') and Page 21 ('Enable Speech Viewer') contain headings, instructional text, and multiple screenshots.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML rendering of these sections shows only the headings ('Creating a Keyboard Shortcut' and 'Enable Speech Viewer') with no content following them before the next heading begins.
concerns.location: Sections 'Creating a Keyboard Shortcut' (slide 15) and 'Enable Speech Viewer' (slide 21)

concerns.analysis: Numerous meaningful images—specifically screenshots of software interfaces, settings dialogs, and keyboards—are missing from the HTML output. These images are central to the document's purpose of instructing users on how to use accessibility tools. Missing meaningful images were identified for slides 15, 18, 19, 20, 21, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, and 32. While some slides have their content extracted into tables, the original visual context is lost, and for many other slides, no content at all is r
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 30 ('Enabling VoiceOver iDevices') contains four screenshots illustrating iOS accessibility settings.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output under the 'Enabling VoiceOver iDevices' heading contains only a repeated text placeholder and no images.

concerns.analysis: Several numbered lists in the source document were incorrectly converted into unordered lists in the JSON output. This occurs on multiple pages where the source explicitly uses '1.', '2.', etc., to indicate a sequence of steps.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 6: '1. Use underline', '2. Can use...'; Page 7: '1. Enter field name' through '4. Save time...'; Page 9: '1. Add radio buttons' through '5. Enter Choice...'; Page 10: '1. Use Preview button' through '3. Select Clear Form'.
concerns.output_quote: Section 6 elements: list_type: 'unordered'; Section 7 elements: list_type: 'unordered'; Section 9 elements: list_type: 'unordered'; Section 10 elements: list_type: 'unordered'.

concerns.analysis: The contact links (web address and email address) present on the final slide of the source PDF (Page 34) are completely missing from the output document. While the company name and phone number are present, the critical URL and email contact information were not captured.
concerns.output_quote: Section 34 (last section) contains only the heading 'Get in touch', 'Thank You!', and paragraphs for 'Ablr' and 'Call: 833-294-2257'.

action_items: Verify and restore missing instructional text and screenshots for slides 15, 21, 29, 30, and 32
Re-insert missing contact information and hyperlinks in the final 'Get in touch' section
Correct list formatting to ensure numbered sequences are preserved
